subsystem: ImportEPUB::ExtractContainer() from the Sigil EPUB editor — the routine that opens an EPUB (a ZIP container) with minizip/zlib's unz* API and extracts every archive entry into a temporary staging directory (m_ExtractedFolderPath). It decodes each stored file name, applies a hand-rolled path-traversal (zip-slip) sanitizer, recreates the directory tree, and streams each entry's bytes to disk.
provenance: Identified by the class/method name ImportEPUB::ExtractContainer and Sigil-specific members (m_FullFilePath, m_ExtractedFolderPath, m_ZipFilePaths, QCodePage437Codec, EPUBLoadParseError), the minizip 64-bit API (unzOpen2_64/unzOpen64, unzGoToFirstFile/unzGoToNextFile, unzGetCurrentFileInfo64, unzOpenCurrentFile/unzReadCurrentFile/unzCloseCurrentFile, unz_file_info64, zlib_filefunc64_def, fill_win32_filefunc64W), and Qt types (QString, QDir, QFile, QFileInfo, QObject::tr). The sample banner /* sample: primevul_train_3332_fixed */ marks this as the post-fix variant of a PrimeVul CWE-22 sample (filename train_0170_cwe22.cpp).
trust_boundary: The EPUB file is attacker-controllable data: an EPUB is an untrusted ZIP downloaded/opened by the user. Everything read from the archive's central directory and local headers is hostile — most importantly the stored entry name (copied into file_name), the general-purpose flag bits (file_info.flag), the stored name length (file_info.size_filename, a 16-bit ZIP field up to 65535), and file_info.uncompressed_size. m_FullFilePath / m_ExtractedFolderPath are locally chosen (trusted). The security question is whether the per-entry name sanitizer and the buffer handling prevent an attacker-crafted archive from writing outside the staging directory or reading out of bounds.

what_the_code_does.explanation: The method takes no parameters and works on member state. res holds the minizip status codes. It lazily constructs a cached QCodePage437Codec (cp437) used later to decode IBM-437-encoded names. It then opens the EPUB as a ZIP archive: on Windows it installs a wide-char file-function table via fill_win32_filefunc64W and calls unzOpen2_64 on the native wide path (note c_str() on a temporary std::wstring, valid only for the duration of the call); on other platforms it calls unzOpen64 on the UTF-8 native path. zfile is the minizip archive handle for all subsequent iteration and reads.

If the archive could not be opened, an EPUBLoadParseError exception is thrown carrying the file path. Otherwise unzGoToFirstFile positions the cursor at the first central-directory entry; res captures its status. The main extraction loop runs only if positioning succeeded (UNZ_OK).

For each entry a fixed stack buffer char file_name[MAX_PATH] is zero-initialized and an unz_file_info64 struct is declared to receive the entry metadata. unzGetCurrentFileInfo64 fills file_info and copies the stored name into file_name, told the destination capacity is MAX_PATH. The return value is discarded. qfile_name is then built from file_name via QString::fromUtf8, which reads file_name as a NUL-terminated C string. cp437_file_name is declared for the alternate-encoding path below.

ZIP general-purpose bit 11 signals a UTF-8 name. If it is NOT set, the code assumes legacy IBM code page 437 and decodes file_name a second time through the cp437 codec into cp437_file_name (again treating file_name as a NUL-terminated C string). Both flag and the raw name bytes are attacker-controlled. Empty names are skipped by the isEmpty guard so a nameless entry is ignored.

This is the zip-slip sanitizer. original_path keeps the raw name for error messages. If the name contains any backslash it is flagged as evil, and all backslashes are stripped while a leading '/' is prepended. If the (backslash-stripped, slash-prefixed) name contains the substring "/../" it is flagged evil, and "/../" occurrences are collapsed to "/". Finally every leading '/' is stripped so the name cannot be absolute. The intent: after this, qfile_name should be a relative path with no upward traversal, safe to append to the staging directory. Detection (the evil flags) is the real security boundary; the string edits are cosmetic normalization.

The identical sanitizer is applied to the cp437-decoded name. Crucially, the evil-or-corrupt check and the resulting abort happen HERE, before any filesystem operation: if either name contained a backslash or a "/../" segment, the current file and the archive are closed and an exception is thrown. This ordering (reject before mkpath/open/write) is correct and is what makes the traversal defense effective in the common cases.

The destination path is composed by concatenating the trusted staging folder, a separator, and the sanitized relative name. A QFileInfo derives path components. If the entry's stored uncompressed_size is 0 and the name ends with '/', it is treated as a directory: mkpath creates it and the loop continues. Otherwise the parent directory chain is created (mkpath on qfile_info.path()) and the entry name is recorded in m_ZipFilePaths (preferring the cp437 variant when present). Note the directory-vs-file decision trusts the attacker-supplied uncompressed_size and the name suffix.

The current archive entry is opened for decompression; failure closes the archive and throws. A QFile is opened at file_path with WriteOnly|Truncate; failure closes both handles and throws. The payload is then streamed in BUFF_SIZE chunks: unzReadCurrentFile inflates into the fixed buff and returns the number of bytes actually produced (bounded by BUFF_SIZE), which entry.write persists. The read count is used as the write length, so the write stays within buff; there is no length arithmetic here that could overflow the buffer.

After the write loop the output file is closed. A negative read signals a decompression error and triggers cleanup + throw. Closing the current file returns UNZ_CRCERROR if the stored CRC did not match — treated as fatal. The comment explicitly states the code does NOT compare bytes-written against the declared uncompressed_size, delegating integrity entirely to the CRC. Finally, when a distinct cp437 name exists, the just-written file is copied to a second path built from cp437_file_name (which was sanitized identically), so the copy destination inherits the same protection.

The loop advances to the next entry, continuing while unzGoToNextFile returns UNZ_OK. Normal termination is res == UNZ_END_OF_LIST_OF_FILE; any other terminal status is treated as an error (close + throw). On the success path the archive handle is closed once at the end. The cleanup on each throw path closes zfile, so there is no obvious double-close or leak of the archive handle.

what_could_go_wrong.explanation: Out-of-bounds stack read (CWE-125). The code passes MAX_PATH as both the buffer address family and the capacity, then treats file_name as a NUL-terminated C string in QString::fromUtf8(file_name) and cp437->toUnicode(file_name). minizip's unzGetCurrentFileInfo64 writes a terminating NUL only when the stored name length is strictly LESS than the supplied buffer size; when the ZIP central-directory size_filename (a 16-bit field, up to 65535) is >= MAX_PATH, it copies exactly MAX_PATH bytes and does NOT terminate. Because the entire buffer is filled with attacker bytes, the initial {0} provides no in-bounds terminator, so both fromUtf8 and the cp437 codec scan past file_name[MAX_PATH-1] into adjacent stack memory until they hit a stray zero — a crash and/or a small stack-memory disclosure folded into the extracted file name. The invariant the implementation must uphold is that file_name is always NUL-terminated within MAX_PATH; passing MAX_PATH-1 (and forcing file_name[MAX_PATH-1]=0), or using the returned size_filename with an explicit length, is required.

Incomplete traversal canonicalization (CWE-22, defense-in-depth gap). The traversal test only recognizes the exact substring "/../" — it requires BOTH a leading and a trailing slash. A name whose only up-level component is a TRAILING or bare ".." — e.g. "dir/.." or "." -> ".." — is not flagged ("/dir/.." and "/.." contain no "/../") and survives to path composition as "dir/.." / "..". In this specific code that residual path resolves to a directory and the subsequent WriteOnly open fails (throwing), so it is not a file-write escape, but the sanitizer's guarantee is weaker than it appears and is fragile under refactoring (e.g. if a future caller used qfile_name for mkpath of a file, or on platforms where the parent resolves differently). The single-pass replace("/../","/") is also non-recursive, but since any pre-collapse "/../" already sets the evil flag, that does not by itself defeat the gate. The invariant: reject or fully canonicalize every "."/".." path component, not only the "/../" infix; the robust check is to resolve the final path and verify it is contained within m_ExtractedFolderPath (canonical-prefix check).

Unchecked return value (CWE-252). unzGetCurrentFileInfo64 returns a status but it is discarded. If it fails (UNZ_ERRNO / corrupt entry), file_info is left partially or wholly uninitialized (unz_file_info64 is a plain stack struct, not zeroed here), yet file_info.flag (line 31) and file_info.uncompressed_size (line 84) are then read to drive control flow. file_name being {0} keeps the name empty on total failure (handled by the isEmpty guard), but reading uninitialized file_info fields is undefined and could misclassify an entry as a directory or mis-branch the cp437 decode. The invariant: verify the call returned UNZ_OK before trusting any field of file_info.

Over-trust of attacker-controlled metadata (CWE-20). The directory-vs-file decision and the deliberate omission of a size check both rely on ZIP header fields the attacker sets. file_info.uncompressed_size == 0 is used to classify directories, and the comment at lines 130-131 states the code intentionally does not compare bytes actually written against the declared uncompressed_size, delegating all integrity to the CRC. A crafted archive can declare uncompressed_size=0 for a real payload or otherwise desynchronize declared vs actual sizes; correctness (and any downstream code that trusts m_ZipFilePaths sizes) then depends solely on CRC verification firing. Impact is limited (a mismatched entry typically fails CRC and throws), so this is low severity, but the reliance on unvalidated size metadata should be confirmed. The invariant: treat all unz_file_info64 size/flag fields as untrusted and cross-check written bytes.

Symlink-follow / TOCTOU on write (CWE-59, latent, low). The file is opened by path with WriteOnly|Truncate without O_NOFOLLOW-equivalent protection. This code does not itself create symlinks from archive entries (it always writes regular files), so an in-archive symlink attack is not directly reachable here; but if any component of file_path is a pre-existing symlink on disk (planted out of band, or the staging directory is shared/predictable), the write follows it and truncates/overwrites the target outside the staging tree. The path sanitizer normalizes the name but does not defend against filesystem-level symlinks in the destination. The invariant: create the staging directory privately and open output files without following symlinks, or verify the real (canonicalized) parent stays inside m_ExtractedFolderPath at open time.

summary: This is the post-fix (zip-slip hardened) Sigil EPUB extractor. The path-traversal defense is largely effective: any backslash or "/../" segment is detected and the extraction aborts BEFORE any filesystem operation, and leading slashes are stripped to block absolute paths, so no straightforward directory escape or overflow of the write buffer remains. The sharpest residual issue is a stack out-of-bounds read (CWE-125): file_name[MAX_PATH] is consumed as a NUL-terminated string, but minizip does not NUL-terminate when the stored name length reaches MAX_PATH, so an archive with a >=MAX_PATH-byte entry name reads past the buffer. Secondary concerns are the sanitizer's substring-only "/../" check (misses trailing/bare ".."), a discarded unzGetCurrentFileInfo64 status, and reliance on attacker-supplied size metadata.

reviewed_anchors.reason: No-parameter member method; entry point, no direct contract of its own.
Lazily-initialized member codec cache; ownership/lifetime managed elsewhere, not leaked within this function.
Windows file-function table initialized by trusted library call; not attacker-influenced.
Opens the (locally chosen) EPUB path; c_str() is on a temporary but consumed synchronously within the call, so no dangling.
Correct open-failure guard with throw.
Iteration cursor; status captured in res and validated at loop exit.
Fixed-size buffer consumed as a C string without guaranteed NUL termination — OOB read.
Attacker-controlled metadata struct (flag, uncompressed_size, size_filename) read without validating the fill call.
Return value discarded; MAX_PATH capacity does not force termination.
UTF-8 general-purpose bit test; selects decoder only, no memory-safety contract itself.
Second consumer of the possibly non-terminated file_name buffer; folded into the OOB-read finding.
Correctly skips empty/nameless entries.
Any backslash sets the evil flag and aborts; robustly closes Windows-separator traversal tricks.
Substring-only check misses trailing/bare ".."; incomplete canonicalization.
Strips all leading '/', neutralizing absolute and "//" paths correctly.
Mirror of the qfile_name sanitizer; same properties, covered by the traversal finding.
Correctly rejects flagged names BEFORE any mkpath/open/write and cleans up handles.
Destination built from sanitized relative name plus trusted base; escape blocked by the gate.
Directory/file classification and skipped size verification trust attacker metadata.
Creates directories from an already-sanitized/validated relative path.
Bookkeeping list of sanitized names; no memory-safety contract.
Checked; failure closes archive and throws.
Opens by path without symlink protection — latent CWE-59/TOCTOU.
unzReadCurrentFile is bounded by BUFF_SIZE and the returned count is used as the write length; no overflow.
Correctly detects decompression read errors.
CRC integrity gate handled as fatal.
Copy destination built from the identically-sanitized cp437 name; inherits traversal protection.
Terminal-status validation and single archive close; no double-close/leak observed.

findings.title: Out-of-bounds stack read: file_name treated as NUL-terminated when archive name length reaches MAX_PATH
findings.anchor: char file_name[MAX_PATH] = {0}; unzGetCurrentFileInfo64(..., file_name, MAX_PATH, ...); QString::fromUtf8(file_name) / cp437->toUnicode(file_name)
findings.where_it_lives: corpus/train/train_0170_cwe22.cpp (consumers here; termination behavior in minizip unzGetCurrentFileInfo64, file not provided)
findings.invariant: file_name must be NUL-terminated within its MAX_PATH bytes before being passed to any C-string API; either reserve one byte (pass MAX_PATH-1 and set file_name[MAX_PATH-1]=0) or bound the decode with the returned size_filename.
findings.failure_mode: An attacker sets a ZIP entry's stored name length (size_filename, up to 65535) to >= MAX_PATH. minizip fills all MAX_PATH bytes and omits the terminating NUL; QString::fromUtf8(file_name) and cp437->toUnicode(file_name) then scan past file_name[MAX_PATH-1] into adjacent stack memory until a stray zero, causing a crash or leaking neighboring stack bytes into the constructed file name.
findings.what_to_confirm: Confirm the linked minizip version does not NUL-terminate when size_filename >= buffer size (the standard unzip.c copies exactly fileNameBufferSize bytes and only writes the NUL when size_filename < buffer size), and that no MAX_PATH-1 slack is reserved here.

findings.title: Incomplete path-traversal canonicalization: only the "/../" infix is detected, missing trailing/bare ".."
findings.invariant: Every "."/".." path component must be rejected or fully resolved; the safe check is to canonicalize the final path and verify it stays within m_ExtractedFolderPath, not to match a single "/../" substring.
findings.failure_mode: Names whose only up-level part is a trailing or bare ".." (e.g. "dir/.." -> "/dir/..", or ".." -> "/..") contain no "/../" substring, so they are not flagged and survive sanitization. In this exact code such paths resolve to a directory and the WriteOnly open fails (throwing), so there is no direct write escape today, but the sanitizer's guarantee is narrower than intended and is fragile to any future reuse of the name (e.g. for mkpath of a file component). The single-pass replace("/../","/") is also non-recursive.
findings.what_to_confirm: Confirm no code path uses qfile_name/cp437_file_name for a filesystem write after only substring sanitization, and consider replacing the substring gate with a canonical-prefix containment check against m_ExtractedFolderPath.

findings.title: Discarded unzGetCurrentFileInfo64 status leaves file_info fields potentially uninitialized
findings.bug_class: Unchecked return value / use of uninitialized data
findings.invariant: The return value must be checked for UNZ_OK before reading any field of file_info (which is not zero-initialized).
findings.failure_mode: If the call fails on a corrupt entry, file_info is left with indeterminate contents, yet file_info.flag (line 31) selects the decoder and file_info.uncompressed_size (line 84) drives directory/file classification. Reading these uninitialized fields is undefined behavior and can misclassify an entry; file_name stays empty ({0}) so the name check masks total failure but not partial-fill cases.
findings.what_to_confirm: Verify whether the call's status is (or should be) checked and whether unz_file_info64 is zero-initialized before use.

findings.title: Directory/file discrimination and integrity rely on attacker-controlled ZIP size metadata
findings.invariant: All unz_file_info64 size/flag fields are untrusted; extraction correctness must not depend on them without cross-checking actual decompressed bytes.
findings.failure_mode: An attacker sets uncompressed_size and the name suffix independently of the real payload; the comment at lines 130-131 confirms the code deliberately skips comparing written bytes against uncompressed_size and trusts only the CRC. A desynchronized entry that still passes CRC (or a zero-size declaration for a real payload) can mislabel entries, though impact is limited because a genuine mismatch typically fails CRC and throws.
findings.what_to_confirm: Confirm CRC verification is always reached for file entries and consider validating written size against the declared uncompressed_size.

findings.title: Output file opened by path without symlink protection (latent TOCTOU)
findings.bug_class: Link following / TOCTOU on destination path
findings.invariant: The real (canonicalized) parent of the write target must be confirmed inside a private m_ExtractedFolderPath at open time, and writes should not follow symlinks.
findings.failure_mode: The path sanitizer normalizes the archive name but does not defend against filesystem-level symlinks. If any component of file_path is a pre-existing symlink (staging directory shared/predictable, or a symlink planted out of band), the WriteOnly|Truncate open follows it and overwrites a target outside the staging tree. This code cannot itself materialize symlinks from archive entries, so the vector is indirect and low severity.
findings.what_to_confirm: Confirm m_ExtractedFolderPath is created privately per import and whether the platform opens follow symlinks for the composed file_path.

audit_checklist: Verify the linked minizip's unzGetCurrentFileInfo64 NUL-terminates file_name only when size_filename < MAX_PATH, and add reserved slack (MAX_PATH-1 + explicit terminator) or length-bounded decoding before QString::fromUtf8/cp437->toUnicode.

Replace the substring "/../" gate with a canonical-prefix containment check: resolve the final path and assert it is inside m_ExtractedFolderPath, covering trailing/bare ".." and non-recursive-replace edge cases.
Check the return status of unzGetCurrentFileInfo64 (and zero-initialize unz_file_info64) before reading file_info.flag / file_info.uncompressed_size.
Confirm CRC verification is always executed for file entries and consider validating bytes-written against the declared uncompressed_size instead of trusting the CRC alone.
Ensure the staging directory is created privately and output files are opened without following symlinks (O_NOFOLLOW-equivalent) to close the CWE-59/TOCTOU window.
Confirm the QCodePage437Codec allocation at line 6 is owned/freed appropriately over the object's lifetime and not leaked on repeated imports.
